sourcePolicy: Enterprise routing profiles map task shapes to the safest useful Codex surfaces. They are runtime-permitted routing guidance for clear, non-trivial prompt matches, not hidden execution hooks: matching specialist agents are named visibly and may be spawned only when the current Codex runtime permits subagent delegation, which current Codex releases require the user to ask for explicitly. Matching skills are mandatory when triggered, MCP use is bounded by availability and approval mode, and destructive, credentialed, publishing, deployment, database, and broad filesystem actions remain approval-gated.

profiles.title: Repository map before broad change
profiles.trigger: Broad implementation, refactor, unfamiliar repository, architecture question, or ownership uncertainty.
evidence: Relevant files and ownership boundaries are listed before editing.
Implementation scope names files or modules likely to change.
profiles.boundary: Keep exploration read-only where possible; write-heavy implementation stays in the main thread unless the user explicitly splits write scopes.
profiles.durability: Reusable repo policy and CLI routing-board evidence.
profiles.validationGate: npm run validate:routing and a visible file/module scope before implementation.
profiles.rollback: Remove or narrow this profile in catalog/routing-profiles.json and rerun npm run validate:routing.

profiles.title: Current docs and version-sensitive facts
profiles.trigger: OpenAI/Codex behavior, library APIs, standards, product docs, or any current/version-sensitive claim.
evidence: Official or primary-source references are checked before implementation.
Version-sensitive claims name the source used.
profiles.boundary: Do not rely on memory or guesses when official docs are available and the fact can drift.
profiles.primarySurface: official docs, Context7, and docs citations in the final report
profiles.durability: Per-task evidence; durable docs only when behavior changes.
profiles.validationGate: Source URLs or official docs surfaces are listed before relying on version-sensitive claims.
profiles.rollback: Remove the citation or docs-dependent claim and fall back to repository evidence.

profiles.title: Context surface decision
profiles.trigger: A change might belong in prompts, AGENTS.md, config profiles, skills, plugins, MCP, hooks, memory, rules, or docs.
evidence: The chosen surface names durability, owner, privilege, validation, and rollback.
Docs and validators cover the changed surface.
profiles.boundary: Do not move secrets, credentials, account automation, or broad permissions into durable global instructions.
profiles.primarySurface: AGENTS.md, skills, config templates, plugin metadata, docs, or catalog files
profiles.durability: Durable only when placed in template/config/docs; otherwise thread-local.
profiles.privilegeDelta: none by default; approval mode and connector exposure must stay explicit.
profiles.validationGate: Changed surface is named with owner, durability, privilege, validation, and rollback.
profiles.rollback: Revert the selected surface change with an explicit scoped patch and rerun its validator.

profiles.title: Bug or failing test root cause
profiles.trigger: Bug report, regression, failing test, failing CI, unclear behavior, or production-like incident.
evidence: Reproduction or failure evidence is captured before the fix.
A targeted verification command passes after the fix.
profiles.boundary: Do not patch by guesswork or weaken tests, types, auth, validation, or error handling to get green output.
profiles.primarySurface: reproduction command output, failing file, and targeted verifier
profiles.durability: Per-incident evidence plus tests or validators when behavior changes.
profiles.privilegeDelta: none until a scoped fix is implemented.
profiles.validationGate: Repro command fails before fix or root cause is otherwise evidenced; targeted check passes after fix.
profiles.rollback: Back out only the scoped fix files and restore the failing reproduction as evidence.

profiles.title: Bounded feature implementation
profiles.trigger: Add, build, implement, or extend a bounded product or code feature.
evidence: Feature scope, non-goals, and verification gates are explicit.
Focused tests or equivalent project checks cover the changed behavior.
profiles.boundary: Do not add dependencies, change public APIs, or touch auth/billing/deploy behavior without explicit approval.
profiles.primarySurface: implementation files, focused tests, docs, and validators
profiles.durability: Durable source change with matching docs/tests when user-facing.
profiles.privilegeDelta: workspace writes only unless explicitly expanded.
profiles.validationGate: Feature scope, non-goals, changed files, and verification commands are visible.
profiles.rollback: Revert the feature patch as a scoped change; keep unrelated user changes untouched.

profiles.title: Frontend, UI, and browser evidence
profiles.trigger: Frontend build, redesign, UI polish, accessibility, rendered behavior, screenshots, responsive layout, or browser errors.
evidence: Rendered UI is checked with browser evidence when available.
Accessibility, overflow, console errors, and responsive states are considered.
profiles.boundary: Do not claim UI quality from code inspection alone when browser verification is available.
profiles.primarySurface: browser evidence, screenshots, accessibility checks, and frontend files
profiles.durability: Durable UI source change plus captured evidence for verification.
profiles.privilegeDelta: browser/tool access remains approval-gated when configured that way.
profiles.validationGate: Rendered evidence or a clear reason browser verification was unavailable.
profiles.rollback: Revert the UI patch and rerun browser or screenshot checks.

profiles.title: Security-sensitive code and abuse paths
profiles.trigger: Auth, authorization, input validation, secrets, API routes, database access, permissions, crypto, or threat modeling.
evidence: Security review stays read-only until a scoped fix is assigned.
Secret scans or equivalent checks run before release-sensitive handoff.
profiles.boundary: Never print secrets, broaden permissions, or bypass auth/validation to make checks pass.
profiles.primarySurface: read-only security evidence, scoped fixes, and secret-scan output
profiles.durability: Durable only through reviewed guardrail, validation, or documentation changes.
profiles.privilegeDelta: read-only review first; no credential or permission expansion.
profiles.validationGate: Security-sensitive changes include a secret scan or explicit equivalent.
profiles.rollback: Back out only the scoped security patch and keep credentials out of artifacts.

profiles.title: MCP connector or tool exposure change
profiles.trigger: Enable, disable, add, troubleshoot, or change MCP servers, app connectors, tool allowlists, auth, or timeouts.
evidence: Connector risk, auth boundary, enabled tools, and approval mode are documented.
Authenticated/account/database connectors remain disabled unless explicitly needed.
profiles.boundary: Do not enable account, database, production, or broad filesystem connectors silently.
profiles.primarySurface: catalog/mcp-servers.json, config templates, and MCP docs
profiles.durability: Durable only in catalog/config templates; live connectors remain session/config controlled.
profiles.privilegeDelta: disabled-by-default account/database connectors unless explicitly enabled.
profiles.validationGate: npm run validate:mcp plus documented auth, approval, and rollback notes.
profiles.rollback: Set connector enabled=false or remove the catalog/config entry, then restart Codex.

profiles.title: Release, package, push, or publish readiness
profiles.trigger: Commit, push, PR, tag, release, package, deploy, artifact cleanup, or publication readiness.
evidence: Git status, staged diff, release notes, version, artifacts, and secret scan are checked.
Push, tag, release, deploy, and destructive cleanup require explicit user approval.
profiles.boundary: Do not commit, push, publish, deploy, delete, or rotate credentials unless the user explicitly asks.
profiles.primarySurface: git status, staged diff, package metadata, changelog, release notes, and secret scan
profiles.durability: Durable release metadata and git history only after explicit user approval.
profiles.privilegeDelta: commit, push, tag, release, publish, deploy, and cleanup stay gated.
profiles.validationGate: npm run validate, release checks, gitleaks, git diff --check, and staged diff review.
profiles.rollback: Use normal git/release rollback procedures only after explicit scoped approval.

profiles.title: SEO and web quality
profiles.trigger: Crawlability, metadata, structured data, sitemap, hreflang, canonical URL, Core Web Vitals, or web quality audit.
evidence: SEO claims are tied to inspectable markup, routes, rendered output, or official guidance.
Performance and accessibility findings include reproducible checks when available.
profiles.boundary: Do not promise rankings, automate backlinks, or use credentialed Search Console access without explicit approval.
profiles.primarySurface: rendered markup, route metadata, sitemap/canonical data, and browser evidence
profiles.durability: Durable source/docs changes only; external search-console actions stay gated.
profiles.privilegeDelta: browser/read-only evidence by default; credentialed tools require approval.
profiles.validationGate: SEO/performance claims cite inspectable markup, rendered evidence, or official guidance.
profiles.rollback: Revert affected metadata or UI files and rerun the audit checks.

profiles.title: Documentation, ADRs, and release notes
profiles.trigger: Docs coverage, stale docs, README changes, ADRs, release notes, public copy, or setup guides.
evidence: Docs are updated with the code or behavior they describe.
Localized docs and README alignment checks pass when affected.
profiles.boundary: Do not claim external publication or hosted availability without verifying the public URL or release state.
profiles.primarySurface: README files, docs, changelog, release notes, and docs validators
profiles.durability: Durable documentation and public-facing release notes.
profiles.privilegeDelta: workspace writes only; publication stays gated.
profiles.validationGate: README/docs locale alignment and relevant docs validators pass.
profiles.rollback: Revert the docs patch for the affected surface and rerun docs checks.

profiles.title: Codex Chef starter health and drift
profiles.trigger: Install health, global setup drift, catalog alignment, skill pressure, MCP status, or Codex Chef diagnostics.
evidence: codex:status, verify:install:runtime, or repair preview reports the installed state.
Managed file, agent, MCP, and curated skill counts are visible.
profiles.boundary: Repair may back up and update managed files, but it must not delete user skills, secrets, local state, or unrelated configuration.
profiles.primarySurface: codex status scripts, runtime verifier, repair preview, and managed-file manifest
profiles.durability: Durable starter health evidence through scripts and managed templates.
profiles.privilegeDelta: read-only diagnostics first; repair writes only after explicit apply.
profiles.validationGate: codex:status, verify:install:runtime, or repair preview names the installed state and drift.
profiles.rollback: Restore managed files from installer backup only with explicit approval.
